# n-dx

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 627
- **Lines of code**: 186,861
- **Languages**: TypeScript (512), CSS (30), JSON (27), Other (20), JavaScript (19)
- **Zones**: 11 (11 with descriptions)
- **Git**: feature/from-recommend @ 536ec50
- **Analyzed**: 2026-02-18T02:46:43.078Z

## Architecture Zones

### Autonomous Agent Core (`autonomous-agent`)

Primary agent execution engine handling task analysis, lifecycle management, and autonomous workflow orchestration.

- Files: 76
- Cohesion: 0.93, Coupling: 0.07
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/schema/index.ts, packages/hench/src/schema/v1.ts, packages/hench/src/tools/exec-shell.ts, packages/hench/src/tools/git.ts, packages/hench/src/types/index.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/autonomous-agent/context.md`

### Dashboard Components (`dashboard-components`)

Comprehensive UI component library providing data visualization, navigation, and interactive elements for the web dashboard.

- Files: 92
- Cohesion: 0.95, Coupling: 0.05
- Entry points: packages/web/src/viewer/components/guide.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/components/logos.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/components/theme-toggle.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/types.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/utils.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/dashboard-components/context.md`

### Execution Gateway (`execution-gateway`)

Cross-package integration layer managing PRD operations, process execution, and tool dispatch for the autonomous agent.

- Files: 14
- Cohesion: 0.60, Coupling: 0.40
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/prd/ops.ts, packages/hench/src/process/index.ts, packages/hench/src/tools/index.ts, packages/hench/src/tools/rex.ts, packages/hench/src/validation/completion.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/execution-gateway/context.md`

### Foundation Layer (`foundation-layer`)

Shared infrastructure providing Claude API abstraction, authentication, and common utilities for all domain packages.

- Files: 28
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/foundation-layer/context.md`

### Orchestration Layer (`orchestration-layer`)

Command-line entry points and configuration management that coordinate package execution without direct library dependencies.

- Files: 6
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/orchestration-layer/context.md`

### Rex PRD Management CLI (`packages-rex:rex-cli`)

A self-contained CLI tool and MCP server for managing hierarchical product requirements documents, including code analysis, tree operations, validation, persistence, and AI-agent workflow integration.

- Files: 36
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-rex:rex-cli/context.md`

12 files, primarily TypeScript

### PRD Management UI (`prd-management-ui`)

Specialized interface components for PRD tree manipulation, task execution, and project analysis workflows.

- Files: 28
- Cohesion: 0.93, Coupling: 0.07
- Entry points: packages/web/src/viewer/components/prd-tree/execution-panel.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/components/prd-tree/smart-add-input.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/views/analysis.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/views/prd.ts

### Presentation Gateway (`presentation-gateway`)

Core web server infrastructure providing MCP endpoints, routing, and configuration management.

- Files: 45
- Cohesion: 0.99, Coupling: 0.01
- Entry points: packages/web/src/schema/data-files.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/presentation-gateway/context.md`

### Tool Infrastructure (`tool-infrastructure`)

Agent tooling subsystem providing command guards, file operations, shell execution, and tool dispatch mechanisms.

- Files: 11
- Cohesion: 0.68, Coupling: 0.33
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/agent/tools.ts, packages/hench/src/guard/index.ts, packages/hench/src/tools/dispatch.ts, packages/hench/src/tools/files.ts, packages/hench/src/tools/shell.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/tool-infrastructure/context.md`

## Route Structure

### Server API Routes

**/api/hench/adaptive/** (handleAdaptiveRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-adaptive.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/adaptive/override/:key
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/analysis
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/history
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/apply
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/dismiss/:id
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/lock/:key
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/override
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/unlock/:key
```

**/api/ndx-config** (handleConfigRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-config.ts`

```
GET     /api/ndx-config
GET     /api/projects
POST    /api/projects/switch
```

**/api/hench/** (handleHenchRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-hench.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/templates/:id
GET     /api/hench/config
GET     /api/hench/runs
GET     /api/hench/runs/:id
GET     /api/hench/templates
GET     /api/hench/templates/:id
POST    /api/hench/execute
POST    /api/hench/templates
POST    /api/hench/templates/:id/apply
PUT     /api/hench/config
```

**/api/project** (handleProjectRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-project.ts`

```
GET     /api/project
```

**/api/rex/** (handleRexRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-rex.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/rex/items/:id/requirements/:rid
GET     /api/rex/dashboard
GET     /api/rex/execute/status
GET     /api/rex/items/:id
GET     /api/rex/items/:id/requirements
GET     /api/rex/log
GET     /api/rex/next
GET     /api/rex/prd
GET     /api/rex/proposals
GET     /api/rex/prune/preview
GET     /api/rex/requirements/coverage
GET     /api/rex/requirements/traceability
GET     /api/rex/stats
PATCH   /api/rex/items/:id
PATCH   /api/rex/items/:id/requirements/:rid
PATCH   /api/rex/items/bulk
POST    /api/rex/analyze
POST    /api/rex/batch-import
POST    /api/rex/execute/epic-by-epic
POST    /api/rex/execute/pause
POST    /api/rex/execute/resume
POST    /api/rex/items
POST    /api/rex/items/:id/requirements
POST    /api/rex/items/merge
POST    /api/rex/proposals/accept
POST    /api/rex/proposals/accept-edited
POST    /api/rex/prune
POST    /api/rex/smart-add-preview
```

**/api/sv/** (handleSourcevisionRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-sourcevision.ts`

```
GET     /api/sv/components
GET     /api/sv/context
GET     /api/sv/imports
GET     /api/sv/inventory
GET     /api/sv/manifest
GET     /api/sv/summary
GET     /api/sv/zones
```

```
GET     /api/status
```

```

```

**/api/hench/workflow/** (handleWorkflowRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-workflow.ts`

```
GET     /api/hench/workflow/analysis
GET     /api/hench/workflow/history
POST    /api/hench/workflow/apply
POST    /api/hench/workflow/suggestions/:id
```

### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- Inconsistent gateway naming pattern: autonomous-agent uses 'prd/ops.ts' while presentation-gateway uses 'mcp-deps.ts', reducing architectural clarity [warning]
- God function: handleRexRoute in packages/web/src/server/routes-rex.ts calls 48 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions [warning]

### Patterns

- Architecture successfully implements layered pattern with clean separation: orchestration delegates to CLI, execution bridges domains, foundation provides stable base [info]
- Gateway pattern implementation in execution-gateway serves as architectural firewall, preventing domain package coupling from propagating throughout the system [info]
- No circular dependencies detected between any zones - architecture maintains proper acyclic dependency structure [info]
- Web package zones demonstrate exemplary three-tier architecture with clean unidirectional dependencies and proper separation of concerns [info]

### Relationships

- All cross-zone relationships follow dependency inversion principle - zones depend on abstractions, not concretions [info]
- Cross-zone coupling follows logical data flow patterns: autonomous-agent → execution-gateway (14 imports) and tool-infrastructure → autonomous-agent (7 imports) suggest clear architectural intent [info]
- Tightly coupled modules: packages/web/src/server/routes-rex.ts and packages/web/src/server/types.ts — 129 cross-file calls (unidirectional). Consider extracting shared interface or merging [info]

### Observations

- High cohesion (0.93) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (autonomous-agent)
- High cohesion (0.95) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (dashboard-components)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (foundation-layer)
- Bidirectional coupling: "autonomous-agent" ↔ "execution-gateway" (14+1 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Bidirectional coupling: "dashboard-components" ↔ "prd-management-ui" (4+6 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Size imbalance: largest zone has 92 files vs smallest with 6 — uneven decomposition [info]
- All web zones maintain high cohesion (0.93-0.99) and low coupling, indicating well-designed component boundaries [info]
- Architecture successfully implements the four-tier dependency hierarchy with appropriate coupling levels at each layer [info]
- Cross-zone import patterns show healthy architectural relationships with minimal coupling between major system areas [info]
- Gateway pattern in execution-gateway zone prevents cross-package import sprawl while enabling necessary integrations [info]
- ... and 6 more

### Suggestions

- Analysis state machine lacks centralized validation - adaptive.ts, review.ts, stuck.ts operate independently without state consistency checks [info] (autonomous-agent)
- Lifecycle entry points (cli-loop.ts vs loop.ts) should share common validation logic to prevent behavior divergence between CLI and programmatic execution [warning] (autonomous-agent)
- Consider establishing consistent component naming convention - some use kebab-case (collapsible-section.ts) while others may follow different patterns [info] (dashboard-components)
- Gateway ops.ts becomes single point of failure - consider circuit breaker pattern for rex service calls [info] (execution-gateway)
- Process execution in exec.ts lacks timeout and resource controls, creating risk for runaway autonomous operations [warning] (execution-gateway)
- Consider extracting common error handling patterns from utilities into shared error boundary module [info] (foundation-layer)
- Autonomous operation safety controls are fragmented across zones - consider unified resource limits and safety policy enforcement across orchestration process spawning, execution-gateway process execution, and tool-infrastructure command dispatch [warning]
- Consider documenting the established naming conventions across web package zones (routes-* for handlers, *-panel for workflow UIs) to guide future development [info]
- Gateway pattern implementation inconsistency creates architectural drift risk - standardize naming (ops.ts vs mcp-deps.ts) and enforce dynamic import restrictions through linting rules [info]
- Hub function: walkTree in packages/rex/src/core/tree.ts is called from 20 files (high fan-in expected for this module type) — changes here have wide impact, consider if responsibilities can be narrowed [info]
- ... and 7 more
